steps.title: Locate x = 5 on the horizontal axis
steps.content: On the graph, find $x = 5$ by counting 5 units to the right of the origin along the horizontal axis.

steps.title: Read the y-value where the curve intersects x = 5
steps.content: From the graph, at $x = 5$ the curve crosses the x-axis, so $y = 0$. Therefore $f(5) = 0$.

steps.title: Check each choice against the graph
steps.content:
• A) $f(5) = 2$ — the curve is not at $y=2$ when $x=5$.
  • B) $f(5) = 5$ — the curve is not at $y=5$ when $x=5$.
  • C) $f(5) = -3$ — the curve is not at $y=-3$ when $x=5$.
  • D) $f(5) = 0$ — matches the graph. D is correct.
